jokes.text: Three minutes, 40 seconds... a new personal best.
jokes.setup: Homer timing something with apparent pride in improvement
jokes.punchline: The implication he's timing bathroom activities
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer character comedy - his pride in mundane accomplishments. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: No one will ever know. / Mail call.
jokes.setup: Homer's confident assertion that no one will discover something
jokes.punchline: Immediate interruption proving someone is there
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with immediate contradiction. Classic misdirection structure.

jokes.text: The 'George Foreman Mail Sorter' will let us know.
jokes.setup: Question about mail quality
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous product name/concept for sorting mail
jokes.explanation: Funny absurd product concept playing on George Foreman's branding of everything.

jokes.text: It's where J-Lo hit P. Diddy upside the head with Gary Coleman.
jokes.setup: Describing what makes the spa famous
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd celebrity incident using Gary Coleman as a weapon
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - using a person as a weapon in celebrity violence. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Our attendants can take 10 years off your face... and stick it in your boobs.
jokes.setup: Typical spa promise to make you look younger
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'taking years off' and putting them somewhere else
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay taking a common expression literally with absurd results.

jokes.text: It's also for vain, effeminate men.
jokes.setup: Spa trying to attract male customers
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest description of their target demographic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - advertising honesty taken too far.

jokes.text: Kent Brockman? / Well, if you find this shocking, look at Tom Brokaw.
jokes.setup: Homer recognizing Kent at the spa
jokes.punchline: Kent deflecting by referencing similar real-world anchor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Kent, mixing real and fictional news anchors.

jokes.text: Truly, that was the greatest regeneration.
jokes.setup: Kent's vanity about his appearance
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic assessment of spa treatment
jokes.explanation: Fits Kent's pretentious character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This place blows. I'm gonna go float a Baby Ruth down a mineral bath.
jokes.setup: Bart's boredom with the spa
jokes.punchline: Plan to simulate poop in the fancy mineral baths
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart mischief - classic prank reference that fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Do you like Dr. Seuss? / No. / Then you'll love Dr. Mas-Seuss.
jokes.setup: Kids' entertainment option at spa
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun name for massage therapist
jokes.explanation: Groan-worthy pun but the anti-setup (Bart saying no) makes it work better.

jokes.text: Bad posture has floobled and snozzled your neck.
jokes.setup: Massage therapist in Dr. Seuss style
jokes.punchline: Made-up Seussian words for medical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good Seuss parody with invented words that sound medical but silly.

jokes.text: It just shows you God has a plan for every creature.
jokes.setup: Turtles being used for massage
jokes.punchline: Marge finding deep meaning in absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - finding spiritual meaning in ridiculous circumstances.

jokes.text: [AS JOHNNY CARSON] The American taxpayer!
jokes.setup: Homer just made the taxpayer joke
jokes.punchline: Repeating it in Johnny Carson's voice for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that doesn't add much - repeating the same joke in different voice.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. A naked celebrity. Be cool. Don't stare at his famous wang.
jokes.setup: Marge encountering naked celebrity
jokes.punchline: Her internal monologue about not staring at genitals
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Marge's prudish but curious reaction to celebrity nudity.

jokes.text: Go ahead, look. The whole world already saw it in Nudist Camp Commandant. 'I wore nothing!'
jokes.setup: Celebrity responding to Marge's staring dilemma
jokes.punchline: Reference to absurd nude movie with military title
jokes.explanation: Funny fake movie title combining nudity with war film conventions.

jokes.text: Do not use steam room if you are overweight or have a heart condition. [GULPS] Wait a minute. I'm not wearing a shirt.
jokes.setup: Homer reading health warnings that clearly apply to him
jokes.punchline: Only concern is his shirtlessness, not the serious health risks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the important warning and focusing on trivial detail.

jokes.text: Who ordered the steamed gentile? / Mm, steamed gentile.
jokes.setup: Homer being rescued from steam room
jokes.punchline: Treating cooked human as food order / Homer appreciating the pun
jokes.explanation: Dark wordplay on 'steamed vegetables' with religious twist. Homer's appreciation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Now, Mr. Simpson, is it possible you're living a double... or triple life that your wife doesn't know about? / Triple? No. Definitely no.
jokes.setup: Standard police question about secret life
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific denial of 'triple' implies double life exists
jokes.explanation: Clever character moment where Homer's denial actually reveals guilt. Great structure.

jokes.text: Where on my badge does it say anything about protecting people? / Uh, second word, chief.
jokes.setup: Wiggum questioning his job responsibilities
jokes.punchline: His badge literally says 'Police' which includes protection
jokes.explanation: Great Wiggum incompetence joke with perfect straight-man response from Lou.

jokes.text: Thanks, Princeton Pete.
jokes.setup: Lou correcting Wiggum's ignorance
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's condescending nickname for the competent officer
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Wiggum's resentment of Lou's competence.

jokes.text: Paris? / No. No, not Paris. I'm never gonna go to Paris.
jokes.setup: Wiggum saying he knows where to find criminal mind expert
jokes.punchline: Random outburst about never visiting Paris
jokes.explanation: Random Wiggum non-sequitur that's absurd but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Each one crazier than the last. Uh, come to think of it... he should be there and this guy, he should be over there. / Wait. You're saying he's crazier than I am? / That's exactly what I'm saying, Decapitating Harry.
jokes.setup: Wiggum organizing prisoners by craziness level
jokes.punchline: Prisoner named 'Decapitating Harry' being offended by ranking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd prisoner name and petty criminal ego.

jokes.text: It beats Disney's California Adventure. / Oh, yeah. / Yeah, that's true.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizing bringing kids to prison
jokes.punchline: Family agreeing prison is better than disappointing Disney park
jokes.explanation: Timely cultural reference - Disney's California Adventure was notoriously disappointing when it opened.

jokes.text: Access denied. / Oh, fine. I gotta use a fork.
jokes.setup: High-tech security system denying access
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's primitive solution to electronic lock
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - using cutlery for electronic security.

jokes.text: Hello, Bart. / Aah! Sideshow Bob! / Oh, come now. We've been through so much together. Just call me Bob. / Aah! Bob!
jokes.setup: Sideshow Bob's casual greeting to his nemesis
jokes.punchline: Bart screaming at both the formal and informal name
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob/Bart dynamic with good escalation of Bart's terror.

jokes.text: This year, I think it's Man of La Mancha. Yeah. Don Quixote, Sancho Panza... Dr. Carrasco, you know, they're all great parts. / Ugh, what a great show. I wish it was today.
jokes.setup: Describing prison musical roles
jokes.punchline: Bob's genuine excitement for theater even in prison context
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's theater obsession overcoming circumstances.

jokes.text: Then you might as well stay with us. We serve the same meat the prisons do.
jokes.setup: Bob needing access to the family
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual admission of terrible cooking
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware joke about Marge's cooking quality.

jokes.text: This man has tried to kill me so much, it's not funny.
jokes.setup: Bart protesting Bob staying with them
jokes.punchline: Claiming repeated murder attempts 'aren't funny' in a comedy show
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the show's comedic treatment of murder attempts.

jokes.text: Dad, I can't believe you're putting my life at risk to save your own. / You'll understand someday when you have kids.
jokes.setup: Bart criticizing Homer's selfishness
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic about parental priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating child endangerment as life lesson about parenting.

jokes.text: And don't try taking it off because it's taped to your leg hair. / And that really hurts.
jokes.setup: Explaining shock collar attachment method
jokes.punchline: Bob's pain from tape on leg hair, not electric shock
jokes.explanation: Funny misdirection - focusing on tape pain rather than shock device.

jokes.text: As gentlemen, surely we can just agree to... Dear God! / Sorry. / Fair enough. But now that we know what it can do, there's no need to... Mother!
jokes.setup: Bob trying to negotiate civilly
jokes.punchline: Repeated shocking interrupting his sophisticated speech
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bob's refined speech being interrupted by crude device.

jokes.text: Wow. You don't even have to point it at him.
jokes.setup: Bob being shocked repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Bart's amazed observation about the remote's sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about the device's effectiveness.

jokes.text: Well, there's Mr. Burns... Fat Tony, the Emperor of Japan, ex-President Bush. / The late Frank Grimes. / PBS, Stephen Hawking... the fat little Dixie Chick. / And the state of Florida.
jokes.setup: Listing Homer's enemies
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd list from local to international to abstract entities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from reasonable enemies to completely absurd ones. Great character moment.

jokes.text: How can one man have so many enemies? / I'm a people person. Who drinks.
jokes.setup: Bob's amazement at Homer's enemy count
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional self-assessment with crucial qualifier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion with economical self-awareness. Great structure and character integration.

jokes.text: This is a normal day? / I just wanted to impress you.
jokes.setup: Bob questioning Homer's unusual behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer admitting he's trying to impress his would-be killer
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment - wanting approval from dangerous criminal.

jokes.text: Didn't last year's king have to abdicate because he married a commoner? / And I'd do it again. But I do miss the Royal Crown Cola.
jokes.setup: Question about Mardi Gras king's abdication
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Royal Crown' as both monarchy symbol and soda brand
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting royal imagery to product placement.

jokes.text: Ah, yes, the Kwik-E-Mart. I haven't been here since I robbed it dressed as Krusty. / My one successful crime.
jokes.setup: Bob reminiscing about past Kwik-E-Mart visit
jokes.punchline: Admitting it was his only successful criminal act
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series history with self-deprecating twist about Bob's criminal incompetence.

jokes.text: Our time is passing, old friend.
jokes.setup: Discussion of refined robbery techniques
jokes.punchline: Bob's melodramatic lament about changing criminal standards
jokes.explanation: Good Bob character moment showing his pretentious nature even about crime.

jokes.text: Uh, if you two country hens are finished clucking... I'd like to buy a copy of Jugs and Ammo.
jokes.setup: Snake interrupting Bob and Apu's conversation
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous magazine combining objectification with weapons
jokes.explanation: Perfect fake magazine title combining two stereotypical male interests.

jokes.text: Well, we've settled that argument. You can't read a magazine and drive.
jokes.setup: Snake presumably crashing while reading
jokes.punchline: Bob's matter-of-fact conclusion about multitasking
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to implied visual gag.

jokes.text: But your car was built in Croatia. It's made from old Soviet tanks. / Just fix it, Mr. Sasswrench.
jokes.setup: Mechanic explaining Homer's car construction
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive nickname for the mechanic
jokes.explanation: Good absurd car backstory with Homer's typical dismissive attitude.

jokes.text: Right, right. I'm counting the pennies in the ashtray. / There's two.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be frugal about repair costs
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small amount of money available
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective poverty joke showing Homer's limited resources.

jokes.text: You're so smart. Shock him. / Odds Bodkins!
jokes.setup: Homer impressed by Bob's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Immediately punishing Bob with shock + Bob's archaic exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer illogic plus Bob's period-appropriate exclamation of pain.

jokes.text: Now who's the sociopath, huh?
jokes.setup: Bob being tortured with electric shocks
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the irony of who's really acting psychotic
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the situation's role reversal.

jokes.text: These are Homer's friends and family. They don't want him dead. They just want him to suffer.
jokes.setup: Explaining why locals are attacking Homer dummy
jokes.punchline: Distinguishing between murder and general malice
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark observation about how Homer's loved ones feel about him.

jokes.text: Once I kill you, everyone will think I'm the real Homer. Ha-ha-ha. / None of this seems odd to you? / Meh.
jokes.setup: Ridiculous plan to replace Homer by killing him
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete indifference to the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely unbothered by obvious plot holes.

jokes.text: Yeah. A kid should be real simple to kill. I'd come up behind him with a knife and slit his throat real quick.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bob's failure to kill Bart
jokes.punchline: Moe's casual, graphic description of child murder
jokes.explanation: Very dark humor that's in character for Moe but pushes boundaries.

jokes.text: Homer, if I could write haikus while skinheads beat me with soap... I can concentrate anywhere.
jokes.setup: Bob's claim he can think despite distractions
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing prison violence while maintaining literary pretensions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob character moment - combining prison brutality with artistic pursuits. Excellent contrast.

jokes.text: Oh, why didn't I take more photos? / Memories / Like the corners of my mind
jokes.setup: Marge lamenting lack of photos of her destroyed possessions
jokes.punchline: Breaking into Barbra Streisand song mid-grief
jokes.explanation: Decent musical reference joke but fairly predictable for this type of situation.

jokes.text: Ha, ha. I thought I could get it while it was in the recharger. / I was wrong.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to grab shock remote while charging
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret after getting shocked
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with typical Homer poor planning.

jokes.text: I don't know how we ever had fun without him.
jokes.setup: Family watching Bob get repeatedly shocked
jokes.punchline: Bart's appreciation for torturing his would-be killer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about finding entertainment in Bob's suffering.

jokes.text: Hey, Pop, where's the crank on this Victrola? / Why, it's the latest model. From now on, the only crank in this house is your Aunt Gladys. / For your information, I'm cranky because I've got polio.
jokes.setup: 1930s family sitcom setup about record player
jokes.punchline: Dark historical disease reference in cheerful sitcom context
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about how period sitcoms would handle serious historical issues.

jokes.text: Tom Shales gave this show a good review and I'm the one in prison.
jokes.setup: Bob watching terrible TV in prison
jokes.punchline: Comparing his imprisonment to TV critic's approval of bad show
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about TV criticism and quality, perfect for intellectual Bob.

jokes.text: Aah! Don't look at me. / Homer Simpson? / Right here.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to hide from potential killer
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing himself when called
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer contradiction - hiding then immediately exposing himself.

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! Good things do happen to bad people.
jokes.setup: Homer being chosen as Mardi Gras king
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest self-assessment while celebrating
jokes.explanation: Great Homer self-awareness combined with celebration.

jokes.text: Marge, you're embarrassing me in front of the drag queen.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concern about the parade
jokes.punchline: Homer's misplaced priorities about social embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Funny Homer priority inversion - caring about drag queen's opinion over safety.

jokes.text: In a related story, Homer Simpson may not have long to live.
jokes.setup: Kent reporting on Homer being Mardi Gras king
jokes.punchline: Casual death threat delivered as news update
jokes.explanation: Good media satire with Kent's casual delivery of ominous news.

jokes.text: This is a way to flush this killer out once and for all. And get drunk on a Tuesday. / Today's Tuesday. And you've had six beers. / But I'm not drunk.
jokes.setup: Homer justifying dangerous parade participation
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact correction of his drinking claims
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Marge's factual corrections to Homer's delusions.

jokes.text: So, uh, you're from Tempe? I'm from Chicago. / Whoa, I'm so drunk I'm gonna puke. You ever get that feeling, beautiful?
jokes.setup: Lou trying to work undercover at parade
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's terrible attempt at blending in
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence at undercover work.

jokes.text: America loves its kings, from George III to Larry.
jokes.setup: Homer pontificating about American royalty
jokes.punchline: Pairing historical king with random commoner name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist pairing of historical figure with mundane name.

jokes.text: Phew. Just Italians.
jokes.setup: Homer hearing gunfire and being concerned
jokes.punchline: Relief when it's just ethnic stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotyping for humor, not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Attention, drunken idiots. This is your king. Sacrifice yourselves to slow me down.
jokes.setup: Homer's runaway float heading for danger
jokes.punchline: Commanding crowd to sacrifice themselves for his benefit
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly selfish Homer moment - using royal authority to demand martyrdom.

jokes.text: Hey, how about some beads? / They're not for dudes. / That's cool.
jokes.setup: Person asking for traditional Mardi Gras beads during emergency
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual rejection followed by person's easy acceptance
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about priorities during crisis.

jokes.text: That museum has been nothing but trouble since it opened.
jokes.setup: Homer heading toward Museum of Swordfish
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's matter-of-fact assessment of museum danger
jokes.explanation: Funny absurd premise that a swordfish museum would be dangerous.

jokes.text: I saved a life. And it feels wonderful.
jokes.setup: Bob rescuing Homer from the runaway float
jokes.punchline: Bob's genuine surprise at feeling good about helping
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's capacity for redemption.

jokes.text: And yet, I could just as easily drop you to your death. / Don't be so sure. I got my legs wrapped around your ass pretty tight.
jokes.setup: Bob contemplating whether to save or kill Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude but practical response to death threat
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crude practicality in life-threatening situation.

jokes.text: And yet, I could just as easily drop you to your death. Don't be so sure. I got my legs wrapped around your ass pretty tight.
jokes.setup: Bob contemplating dropping Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude but practical response to threat
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Homer's practical if crude response to mortal danger.

jokes.text: Everything going dark. Like Duff Stout, the beer that made Ireland famous. I love you, Doris.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently dying from gunshot
jokes.punchline: Final words being beer commercial and wrong wife's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer death scene - advertising slogan and wrong wife's name. Brilliantly in character.

jokes.text: Your king needs these stilts. / Jesus is our only king. / Not anymore.
jokes.setup: Homer demanding stilts from street performer
jokes.punchline: Religious objection followed by Homer's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good religious humor with Homer's practical authority claim.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. How is old Grimey?
jokes.setup: Frank Grimes Jr. explaining his father's death
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual inquiry about man he drove to death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to the gravity of his past actions. Great callback.

jokes.text: He happened to like hookers, okay?
jokes.setup: Question about how unmarried Frank Grimes had a son
jokes.punchline: Blunt, embarrassed explanation of father's sex life
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that's more shock value than clever writing.

jokes.text: If I can tranq out just one freak on stilts... I know I've done my job. / You're living the dream, chief.
jokes.setup: Wiggum's low-bar assessment of police success
jokes.punchline: Lou's deadpan encouragement of Wiggum's modest goals
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Lou's dry support of Wiggum's incompetence.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's every parent's dream to outlive their children.
jokes.setup: Bart expressing relief that Homer is alive
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted understanding of parental hopes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark inversion of parental sentiment. Perfect Homer misunderstanding of family dynamics.

jokes.text: I... I can't do it. / Why not? / Well, I guess I've, dear God... grown accustomed to your face.
jokes.setup: Bob unable to kill Bart despite opportunity
jokes.punchline: Referencing My Fair Lady while admitting attachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob character moment combining his theatrical nature with genuine character growth.

jokes.text: I've grown accustomed to his face / And dreams of gouging out his eyes
jokes.setup: Bob starting musical number about Bart
jokes.punchline: Subverting romantic song with violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody combining romantic musical theater with murder fantasies. Very Bob.

jokes.text: Bart, turn down that original cast recording and go to sleep.
jokes.setup: Bob singing elaborate musical number
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it as recorded music from another room
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke treating the musical number as if it's a recording. Perfect fourth wall break.

jokes.text: This isn't a duet. / Sorry.
jokes.setup: Bart attempting to join Bob's solo song
jokes.punchline: Bob correcting the musical theater convention
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about musical theater conventions and Bob's perfectionist nature.

jokes.text: We shall meet again, old friend. But now I must steal away into the night.
jokes.setup: Bob preparing to leave after his character development
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic exit speech for simple departure
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob pretentiousness for mundane action, though not particularly original.
